Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods states CvFatB1 has GenBank accession JF338906.

Source_Species.reason: Paper identifies CvFatB1 as from Cuphea viscosissima.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods and text use Cuphea viscosissima / C. viscosissima for CvFatB1.

Applied_Species.reason: Results and Methods state expression/testing was in E. coli strain K27.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods explicitly say Escherichia coli strain K27.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify E. coli strain K27 (CGSC#5478) with fadD88 mutation.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper calls CvFatB1 an acyl-ACP thioesterase / FatB-type acyl-ACP TE, but does not explicitly use the exact name 'acyl-acyl carrier protein thioesterase B'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CvFatB1 is described as a FatB-type acyl-ACP thioesterase; structural discussion indicates plant acyl-ACP TEs have two hotdog domains, modeled for CvFatB2 and discussed comparatively.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Paper states acyl-ACP thioesterases catalyze the terminal reaction of type II fatty acid synthase and determine fatty acid products.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Introduction explicitly says TE catalyzes the terminal reaction of the type II FAS system.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper explicitly describes TE as catalyzing the terminal reaction and terminating FA biosynthesis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Text states CvFatB1 primarily acts on 8- and 10-carbon fatty acyl-ACPs and belongs to subclass III FatB TEs; FatB enzymes prefer saturated acyl-ACPs.

Primary_Product.reason: Results state CvFatB1 acts on C8/10 acyl-ACPs in E. coli and produces FA profiles enriched in 8- and 10-carbon free fatty acids.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Introduction explicitly states TE hydrolyzes acyl-ACP intermediates to release free FA and terminates FA biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The enzyme directly hydrolyzes acyl-ACP to release the free fatty acid product.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: For CvFatB1, the detected end products in E. coli are medium-chain free fatty acids, mainly C8/C10.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The measured products are free fatty acids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Methods state free FAs accumulated in the growth medium and were extracted from the medium.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction frames fatty acids as energy-dense molecules with biofuel and bioproduct applications.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state CvFatB1 produces predominantly C8/C10 fatty acids; paper also states CvFatB1 is capable of producing three times more FAs than CvFatB2 in E. coli.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are supported: CvFatB1 is subclass III preferring C8/10-ACPs; it gives a distinct medium-chain FA profile in E. coli; and CvFatB1-derived residues introduced into CvFatB2 shifted specificity/productivity toward CvFatB1-like behavior.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported: heterologous expression in E. coli K27, GC-MS analysis of free FAs, domain swapping, site-directed mutagenesis, and immunoblotting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods state constructs were under lacZ promoter control and expression induced by IPTG in E. coli K27.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly say cloned under transcriptional control of the lacZ promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify K27 fadD88 background, M9 minimal medium with glucose, IPTG induction, and 30 °C cultivation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper supports TE as a major determinant/terminal reaction of chain-length output, but does not explicitly call CvFatB1 a 'rate-limiting' step.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Paper states these insights are useful for tailored fatty acid production in engineered organisms and for biofuels/bioproducts.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Paper infers interaction with ACP moiety of the substrate via positive patches, but does not directly demonstrate a specific CvFatB1-ACP protein interaction experimentally.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Methods state mature peptides lacking chloroplast-targeting peptide were codon-optimized and expressed in E. coli; metabolic effects were evaluated in bacteria.

corrections.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods states CvFatB2 has GenBank accession JF338907.

Source_Species.reason: Paper identifies CvFatB2 as from Cuphea viscosissima.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods and text use Cuphea viscosissima / C. viscosissima for CvFatB2.

Applied_Species.reason: Results and Methods state expression/testing was in E. coli strain K27.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods explicitly say Escherichia coli strain K27.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify E. coli strain K27 (CGSC#5478) with fadD88 mutation.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Paper calls CvFatB2 an acyl-ACP thioesterase / FatB-type acyl-ACP TE, but not the exact phrase 'acyl-acyl carrier protein thioesterase B'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CvFatB2 is described as a FatB-type acyl-ACP thioesterase; structural modeling explicitly says predicted tertiary structure contains two hotdog domains.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Paper states acyl-ACP thioesterases function in fatty acid biosynthesis/type II FAS.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Introduction explicitly says TE catalyzes the terminal reaction of the type II FAS system.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Paper explicitly describes TE as catalyzing the terminal reaction and terminating FA biosynthesis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Text states CvFatB2 primarily acts on 14- and 16-carbon fatty acyl-ACPs and belongs to subclass I FatB TEs; FatB enzymes prefer saturated acyl-ACPs.

Primary_Product.reason: Results state CvFatB2 expression gives FA profiles enriched in C14 and C16 free fatty acids.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Introduction explicitly states TE hydrolyzes acyl-ACP intermediates to release free FA and terminates FA biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The enzyme directly hydrolyzes acyl-ACP to release the free fatty acid product.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: For CvFatB2, the measured end products in E. coli are long-chain free fatty acids, mainly C14/C16.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The measured products are free fatty acids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Methods state free FAs accumulated in the growth medium and were extracted from the medium.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction frames fatty acids as energy-dense molecules with biofuel and bioproduct applications.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Discussion support predominant C14/C16 accumulation by CvFatB2 in E. coli and identify V194, V217, N223, R226, R227, I268 as selectivity determinants, with L257, I260, L289 affecting catalytic activity.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each item is supported by Results/Discussion: subclass I preferring C14/C16; domain swapping mapped key determinants especially to fragment III/N-terminus; mutagenesis identified selectivity and activity residues; and 11 substitutions converted CvFatB2 toward CvFatB1-like behavior while some variants gained shorter-chain specificity.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported: heterologous expression in E. coli K27, GC-MS metabolite profiling, domain swapping, site-directed mutagenesis, structural modeling, and western blotting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Methods state constructs were under lacZ promoter control and expression induced by IPTG in E. coli K27.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly say cloned under transcriptional control of the lacZ promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods specify K27 fadD88 background, M9 minimal medium with glucose, IPTG induction, and 30 °C cultivation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Paper supports TE as a major determinant/terminal reaction of product profile, but does not explicitly call CvFatB2 a 'rate-limiting' step.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Paper states these findings are useful for tailored fatty acid production in engineered organisms and advanced biofuels/bioproducts.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Paper proposes ACP moiety binding via positive patches, but this is inferred/model-based rather than directly demonstrated interaction data.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Central mechanistic conclusions indeed focus mainly on CvFatB2 mutagenesis, and Results/Discussion describe two functional structural regions: acyl-binding cavity in N-terminal hotdog domain and positive surface patches likely involved in ACP binding.

corrections.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.
